title: National Early Warning System for Salmonella Foodborne Outbreaks
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     a     leading     cause     of     foodborne     illness     worldwide  ,   posing     a     major     challenge     to     public     health     and     food     safety     systems  . Rapid   detection     and     response   are   essential     to     minimize     the     health  ,   economic  ,   and     trade     impacts     of     outbreaks  .   A   National Early   Warning   System (NEWS)   for     Salmonella     integrates     laboratory     surveillance  , real-  time   data   sharing  ,   and     coordinated     response     mechanisms     across     agencies  . By   leveraging   modern   molecular     tools  ,   including   real-  time   PCR   and     whole-genome     sequencing  , NEWS   can     identify     contamination     sources     faster     and     initiate     timely     recalls  .   This     proactive     approach     supports     prevention     strategies  ,   strengthens     consumer     trust  ,   and     reduces     the     burden     of     disease  .   Implementing     a     robust   NEWS   requires     investment   in   infrastructure  ,   workforce     training  ,   and     harmonized   data   reporting     across     the     food     supply     chain  .
